findings.claim: Every pre-existing, mechanically-enforced oracle in this repo (evidence-locked-uat's judge.py, gauntlet's lens.schema.json/validate_roster.py) was left untouched by this diff. The PR's runtime effect on 4 of the 5 controls (C1, C2, C3, C5) is prose instructing an agent to write more fields into unenforced templates (contracts.yaml, plan text, SKILL.md guidance) — no code path checks whether the agent actually did.

evidence.ref: Y:/dev/_wt/epistemic-flex-v3 git diff --stat e1f6054 641ff2c: judge.py and lens.schema.json absent from the 53-file changed list

plugins/epistemic-skills/skills/evidence-locked-uat/SKILL.md:33-40 (diff) — adds 'compile...into an expected observation and disconfirming observation' as prose Step 6/7, no corresponding judge.py check
plugins/epistemic-skills/skills/write-goal/SKILL.md:63-76 (diff) — adds Goal control template fields with no validator
plugins/epistemic-skills/skills/gauntlet/SKILL.md:246-250 (diff) — adds validation_kernel as arbitrator obligation, absent from gauntlet/roster/lens.schema.json

falsifier.statement: judge.py, lens.schema.json, or another already-CI-wired oracle contains a check for any of expected/disconfirming observation, authorized_priority/success_proxy, or validation_kernel presence.
falsifier.threshold: any non-zero hit falsifies this finding
falsifier.timeframe: immediate — re-run at HEAD or at next PR revision to this branch

findings.proposed_action: Adjacent-possible move: instead of new prose across 9 SKILL.md files, spend the same afternoon wiring validation_kernel into gauntlet's existing lens.schema.json (already CI-tested via gauntlet/tests/run_tests.py) and adding one field-presence check to judge.py for the UAT preregistration fields. Two cheap edits convert two of the five controls from ceremony to a real gate; leave C1/C2 as documentation until a similar host with teeth exists for them.

findings.claim: The new epistemic-process-trace@1 validator/behavioral-scorer/fixture harness (validate_trace.py, score_behavior.py, run_tests.py x2, 6 gold/bad fixture triads + 8 standalone fixtures, ~700 lines) has zero production consumer: no SKILL.md instructs an agent to emit a trace object, and no skill or CI step feeds a real transcript into the validator. The 'PASS 8/8' / 'PASS 12/12' premises are true only about the checker's ability to distinguish hand-authored gold/bad JSON written by the same PR.

evidence.ref: plugins/epistemic-skills/skills/using-epistemic-skills/evals/epistemic-flexibility/behavioral/score_behavior.py:1-7 — self-declares 'a fixture smoke test, not evidence that the controls improve real-world performance'

the fixtures live only under evals/epistemic-flexibility/behavioral/fixtures/*, authored in the same commit (4f7bb45) as the validator [<- V .github/workflows/epistemic-flexibility.yml:29-32 only runs these two self-tests]

falsifier.statement: any skill invocation path (not the eval harness itself) produces or consumes an epistemic-process-trace@1 object during a real task.
falsifier.threshold: any hit outside using-epistemic-skills/evals/ and the reference doc falsifies this finding
falsifier.timeframe: immediate, and re-check at each future release for a wiring commit

findings.proposed_action: Do not stand up a second, parallel structured-record format. Either (a) delete this scaffold until a real skill emits the object it validates, or (b) fold its field vocabulary into the one schema that already has a live production consumption path — decision-ledger's ledger-entry.schema.json / .ledger/entries.jsonl — rather than inventing a second one that never gets real data.

findings.claim: The one genuinely novel, cheap, high-leverage idea in the diff — gauntlet's `validation_kernel` (arbitrator must record what the subject correctly addresses, blocking an adversarial lens from winning by deleting the problem) — rides on gauntlet's already-mechanically-enforced structured-output/arbitration machinery (finding-set@1 schema, lens.schema.json, gauntlet/tests/run_tests.py). This is the strongest 'two existing pieces newly connected' in the PR, but it was shipped as prose only, not wired into that same schema/test suite.

evidence.ref: plugins/epistemic-skills/skills/gauntlet/SKILL.md:246-256 (diff) — validation_kernel and per-tension valid_kernel_a/b fields introduced
grep -rn validation_kernel plugins/epistemic-skills -> single hit, in SKILL.md prose only, absent from roster/lens.schema.json

falsifier.statement: validation_kernel is required in gauntlet's structured-output schema (lens.schema.json or an arbitrator finding-set schema) and checked by gauntlet/tests/run_tests.py.
falsifier.threshold: a match in either file falsifies this finding
falsifier.timeframe: immediate; re-check at the next revision of this PR

findings.proposed_action: Ship the sideways step: add validation_kernel as a required string field to the arbitrator's structured-output contract and one assertion in gauntlet/tests/run_tests.py. ~10-line cost, converts the single best idea in the PR from ceremony to a real gate — dominates leaving it as unenforced prose on both cost and value axes.

findings.claim: Decision-ledger's C4 (recurrent-failure chain) is the best-built piece of the PR — a real conditional JSON-schema-shaped check with planted negative fixtures that correctly assert FAIL ('recurrent correction without chain', 'chain on decision') — but even it only checks structural presence of non-empty strings, not truthfulness: a fluently invented, causally-wrong failure_chain passes identically to a real one, and the check runs only against 3 static reference examples shipped in the repo, never against a repo's live .ledger/entries.jsonl.

evidence.ref: plugins/epistemic-skills/skills/decision-ledger/reference/validate_examples.py:80-89 — planted negative cases with expected FAIL, genuinely discriminating
plugins/epistemic-skills/skills/decision-ledger/reference/validate_examples.py:25-30 — nonempty()/nonempty_list() only check non-whitespace string presence, no semantic/content check
.github/workflows/epistemic-flexibility.yml:57-58 — CI job runs validate_examples.py against reference/ only, no glob over any repo's real .ledger/entries.jsonl

falsifier.statement: validate_examples.py (or an equivalent CI step) is run against a real, non-reference .ledger/entries.jsonl produced by an actual agent session.
falsifier.threshold: any match indicating live-file validation falsifies the 'reference-only' half of this claim
falsifier.timeframe: immediate; re-check when decision-ledger CI is next modified

findings.proposed_action: Adjacent-possible extension (cheapest real teeth in the whole PR): point validate_examples.py's glob at every repo-local .ledger/entries.jsonl found in CI (not just reference/example-*.json), so the one already-working schema gets applied to real production data instead of only to fixtures the same PR wrote. This is a smaller change than the entire epistemic-process-trace@1 scaffold (F2) and gets real enforcement instead of a self-test.

verdict: The PR is real (53 files, +2436/-27, verified in the worktree) but almost all of its added mass is unwired prose. Of the five controls, only C4 (recurrent-failure chain) has a genuinely discriminating mechanical check — and even that runs only against three static reference examples the same PR authored, never against a real .ledger/entries.jsonl. The parallel epistemic-process-trace@1 validator/behavioral-scorer/fixture harness (~700 lines) has zero production consumer: no SKILL.md emits or reads that object; its 8/8 and 12/12 PASS results only prove the checker can tell apart hand-written gold/bad JSON written in the same commit. The dossier's 'VERIFIED premises' PASS lines are true but narrower than they read — they verify tool self-consistency, not that any of C1/C2/C3/C5 fire during real work. Adjacent-possible reading: the dominant nearby variant is not a rewrite — it is (1) deleting or deferring the standalone trace scaffold, (2) pointing the one working oracle (decision-ledger's schema check) at real live ledger files instead of only static references, and (3) wiring the single best new idea (gauntlet's validation_kernel) into the schema/tests gauntlet already has. All three are 10-30 line edits that reuse machinery already proven in this repo, versus the ~2000 lines of new parallel scaffolding that currently enforces nothing against reality.
hypothesis_supported: H3 (unreachable/never fire) and H5 (inadequate/self-referential oracle) are both strongly supported by [V] evidence: zero SKILL.md references the new trace validator, and none of the pre-existing enforced gates (judge.py, lens.schema.json) were touched. H2 (fluent narrative can satisfy a control) is supported for C1/C2/C3/C5 (no truth-check exists at all) and partially for C4 (structural-only presence check accepts fabricated-but-well-formed chains).
hypothesis_killed: H4 (failure_chain conditional is under-specified/not mechanically validated) is refuted specifically for decision-ledger's schema: validate_examples.py implements the conditional correctly and includes planted negative fixtures that genuinely assert FAIL — the conditional logic itself is well-specified and mechanically checked, just only against static reference data (a distinct, narrower concern captured in F4, not H4's original shape).
fix_or_move: Ship this next, in order of cheapness-to-teeth ratio: (1) point decision-ledger's validate_examples.py glob at real repo-local .ledger/entries.jsonl files in CI, not just reference/example-*.json (F4); (2) add validation_kernel as a required field in gauntlet/roster/lens.schema.json plus one assertion in gauntlet/tests/run_tests.py (F3); (3) defer or delete the epistemic-process-trace@1 validator/behavioral-scorer/fixture scaffold until some skill actually emits that object in production (F2) — its current 8/8 and 12/12 PASS numbers should not be cited as evidence the five controls work, only that the checker script is internally consistent.
